sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Nice, currently sitting at 4th in the league with 57 points, host Stade de Reims, who are positioned 13th with 33 points, in Round 32 at the Allianz Riviera in Nice. This matchup pits a top-tier team against a mid-table side, with Nice looking to solidify their strong standing. The referee, Abdelatif Kherradji, is known for averaging 3.8 yellow cards per game and 0.19 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially disciplined yet firm approach to officiating.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: In their last encounter on January 11, 2025, Nice secured a convincing 4-2 victory away at Stade de Reims, with Gaëtan Laborde earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.5 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 head-to-head matchups, Nice have dominated with 2 home wins and 3 draws, leaving Stade de Reims without a victory. Melvin Bard has been a standout for Nice in these games, starting all 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 7.28, showcasing his consistency at the back.

sections.subtitle: Nice's Form and Key Players
sections.text: Nice have scored 60 goals this season, ranking 5th in the league, and boast the league's best record for headers scored with 12, holding the 1st spot in that category. They excel in ground duels, winning 51.94% (4th in the league), and in interceptions per game with 10.75 (1st in the league). Key player Evann Guessand leads the attack with 11 goals and 7 assists, surpassing his expected goals of 8.13. Gaëtan Laborde, with 9 goals, remains a threat despite underperforming his expected goals of 10.20. In-form midfielder Jonathan Clauss, averaging a 7.54 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches, has contributed 4 assists recently, while Tom Louchet stands out with a league rank of 2 for aerial duels won at 78.6%.

sections.subtitle: Stade de Reims' Form and Key Players
sections.text: Stade de Reims have struggled comparatively, but Keito Nakamura has been a bright spot with 11 goals against an expected 8.89, showing clinical finishing. Defender Joseph Okumu, ranked 3rd in the league for long balls percentage at 9.3%, adds a different dimension with his distribution, holding an average rating of 7.11. In-form goalkeeper Yehvann Diouf has been exceptional recently, averaging a 7.96 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches. However, the absence of Junya Ito, who ranks 1st in the league for key passes with 81, could hurt their creative output.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Nice's strength lies in their timing of goals, with 23.08% of their strikes coming between the 31st and 45th minutes, indicating a strong push before halftime. They also create 96 big chances (6th in the league) and enter the penalty area 28.5 times per game (6th in the league). Stade de Reims, conversely, peak late, scoring 20.93% of their goals between the 76th and 90th minutes, suggesting they often fight back in the closing stages. Nice's streak of being the first to score in 5 of their last 6 matches could give them an early edge in this clash.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Discipline
sections.text: Nice are on a run of 3 games without a loss, while Stade de Reims have also gone 3 games without defeat, though that streak is not active. Nice have a tendency for high-scoring games at home, with over 2.5 goals in their last 5 matches, though this streak is inactive. Both teams have historically seen less than 4.5 cards in 8 of their last 10 head-to-heads, pointing to relatively clean encounters. Additionally, their matchups often see fewer than 10.5 corners in 7 of 7 recent games, suggesting a less set-piece heavy affair.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Nice will miss defender Youssouf Ndayishimiye, who has scored 3 goals this season and overperformed his expected goals by 2.0873, potentially weakening their backline. Stade de Reims, without key forward Junya Ito, lose significant creativity with his league-leading 81 key passes. These absences could shift the balance, with Nice possibly feeling the defensive gap more, while Reims struggle to create chances upfront.
